apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of equality to real-life scenarios and case studies.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of inequality on individuals and society.
Students will be able to suggest measures to promote equality in different sectors of Indian society.
analyze: Students will be able to critically analyze the concept of equality and its limitations in the Indian context.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of measures taken by the government to promote equality.
Students will be able to identify the gaps in the implementation of equality and suggest areas for improvement.
remember: Students will be able to recall the concept of equality in the context of Indian polity.
Students will be able to list out the various principles of equality enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
Students will be able to identify examples of equality and inequality in Indian society.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of equality and its significance in a democratic society.
Students will be able to describe the different types of equality (i.e., equal protection of laws, equal opportunity, etc.)
Students will be able to analyze the role of the state in promoting equality in India.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the concept of equality and its importance in a democratic society.
To explain the different types of equality and their applications in Indian society.
To analyze the role of the state in promoting equality in India and its challenges.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the principles of equality enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
To explain the concept of equality and its significance in a democratic society.
To analyze the impact of inequality on individuals and society.
